```text
117	export function renderConsole(v: ConsoleView): string {
118	  const sessJson = JSON.stringify(v.sessions).replace(/</g, "\\u003c");
119	  const dirsJson = JSON.stringify(v.knownDirs).replace(/</g, "\\u003c");
120	  const dirOptions = v.knownDirs
121	    .map((d) => `<option value="${d.replace(/"/g, "&quot;")}">${d.replace(/</g, "&lt;")}</option>`)
122	    .join("");
123	
124	  return `<!DOCTYPE html>
125	<html lang="zh">
126	<head><meta charset="utf-8"><title>attend — console</title><style>${STYLE}</style></head>
127	<body>
128	<div class="side">
129	  <h1>attend <span class="accent">${v.sessions.length} sessions · ${v.briefCount} briefs</span></h1>
130	  <div class="topnav">
131	    <button id="newToggle">+ new</button>
132	  </div>
133	  <div class="newbox" id="newbox">
134	    <select id="ndsel"><option value="">— pick a project dir —</option>${dirOptions}</select>
135	    <input id="ndfree" placeholder="…or paste an absolute path">
136	    <input id="np" placeholder="first message">
137	    <button id="nbtn" class="send" style="color:white;background:#1f2937;border-color:#1f2937">start session ▸</button>
138	    <div class="nmsg" id="nmsg"></div>
139	  </div>
140	  <div id="list"></div>
141	</div>
142	<div class="main">
143	  <div class="head">
144	    <div><div class="t" id="h-title">attend</div><div class="s" id="h-sub">select a session, or + new</div><div class="sig" id="h-sig"></div></div>
145	    <div class="status"><span class="sdot" id="st-dot"></span><span id="st-txt">idle</span></div>
146	  </div>
147	  <div id="msgs"><div class="placeholder" id="ph">Pick a session on the left to see its chat, then type below to continue it — all in the browser.</div></div>
148	  <div class="foot">
149	    <textarea id="input" placeholder="message (Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline)"></textarea>
150	    <button class="splitbtn" id="forkBtn" title="branch this session into a fork (uses your draft as the opening turn)" disabled>fork ⑂</button>
151	    <button class="send" id="send">send</button>
152	  </div>
153	</div>
154	<script>
155	window.__SESSIONS__ = ${sessJson};
156	window.__DIRS__ = ${dirsJson};
157	</script>
158	<script>
159	(function(){
160	  var SESS = window.__SESSIONS__ || [];
161	  var cur = null, es = null, assistantEl = null;
162	  var thinkingEl = null, thinkTimer = null, thinkStart = 0, turnActive = false;
163	  function byId(id){ return document.getElementById(id); }
164	  function el(tag, cls, txt){ var e=document.createElement(tag); if(cls)e.className=cls; if(txt!=null)e.textContent=txt; return e; }
165	  function scroll(){ var m=byId('msgs'); m.scrollTop=m.scrollHeight; }
166	  function clearPh(){ var p=byId('ph'); if(p) p.remove(); }
167	
168	  // --- per-session lifecycle feedback ---
169	  function setStatus(state, txt){ var d=byId('st-dot'); if(d) d.className='sdot '+(state||''); var t=byId('st-txt'); if(t) t.textContent=txt; }
170	  function startThinking(){
171	    clearThinking();
172	    thinkingEl=addMsg('assistant',''); thinkingEl.className='msg assistant thinking';
173	    thinkStart=Date.now();
174	    var tick=function(){ var s=Math.round((Date.now()-thinkStart)/1000); var b=thinkingEl&&thinkingEl.querySelector('.bubble');
175	      if(b) b.textContent = s<20 ? ('working… '+s+'s') : ('working… '+s+'s (still waiting for first response)'); };
176	    tick(); thinkTimer=setInterval(tick,1000); setStatus('working','working…');
177	  }
178	  function clearThinking(){ if(thinkTimer){ clearInterval(thinkTimer); thinkTimer=null; } if(thinkingEl){ thinkingEl.remove(); thinkingEl=null; } }
179	  function endTurn(){ clearThinking(); turnActive=false; var sb=byId('send'); if(sb) sb.disabled=false; setStatus(es?'live':'idle', es?'live':'idle'); }
180	
181	  function sortSessions(){
182	    var mode=(byId('sort')||{}).value||'recent';
183	    if(mode==='priority'){ SESS.sort(function(a,b){ return (b.score||0)-(a.score||0); }); }
184	    else { SESS.sort(function(a,b){ return (a.ageDays==null?1e9:a.ageDays)-(b.ageDays==null?1e9:b.ageDays); }); }
185	  }
186	  function renderSidebar(){
187	    var list=byId('list'); list.innerHTML='';
188	    SESS.forEach(function(s){
189	      var item=el('div','item'+(cur&&cur.sessionId===s.sessionId?' active':''));
190	      item.title = s.reason || '';
191	      // title = brief (easy to locate by task); fall back to first prompt
192	      item.appendChild(el('div','it-title', (s.brief?s.brief.name:(s.title||'(no prompt)'))));
193	      // keep the session's first sentence visible as a subtitle when a brief took the title
194	      if(s.brief && s.title) item.appendChild(el('div','it-firstline', s.title));
195	      var meta=el('div','it-meta');
196	      if(s.pattern && s.pattern!=='unknown') meta.appendChild(el('span','pat '+s.pattern, s.pattern));
197	      meta.appendChild(el('span',null, (s.score!=null?Number(s.score).toFixed(1)+' · ':'')+s.vendor+' · '+s.project+' · '+s.prompts+'p/'+s.actions+'a'+(s.ageDays!=null?(' · '+s.ageDays+'d'):'')));
198	      item.appendChild(meta);
199	      if(s.reason) item.appendChild(el('div','it-reason', s.reason));
200	      item.onclick=function(){ select(s); };
201	      list.appendChild(item);
202	    });
203	  }
204	  function setForkEnabled(on, title){
205	    var b=byId('forkBtn'); if(!b) return; b.disabled=!on; if(title!=null) b.title=title;
206	  }
207	  function addMsg(role, text){ clearPh(); var m=el('div','msg '+role); var b=el('div','bubble',text||''); m.appendChild(b); byId('msgs').appendChild(m); scroll(); return m; }
208	  var toolEls = {}; // tool_use id -> details element (to attach the result later)
209	  function toolPreview(name, input){
210	    try {
211	      if(!input || typeof input!=='object') return '';
212	      if(name==='Bash' && input.command) return String(input.command).split('\\n')[0].slice(0,90);
213	      if(input.file_path) return String(input.file_path);
214	      if(input.path) return String(input.path);
215	      if(input.pattern) return String(input.pattern);
216	      var k=Object.keys(input)[0]; return k?(k+': '+String(input[k]).slice(0,70)):'';
217	    } catch(e){ return ''; }
218	  }
219	  function fmt(v){ try { return typeof v==='string'?v:JSON.stringify(v,null,2); } catch(e){ return String(v); } }
220	  // tc = { name, input, result?, isError? }
221	  function addTool(tc){
222	    clearPh();
223	    var d=el('details','toolc');
224	    var prev=toolPreview(tc.name, tc.input);
225	    d.appendChild(el('summary',null,'⚙ '+tc.name+(prev?(' — '+prev):'')));
226	    if(tc.input!=null && !(typeof tc.input==='object' && Object.keys(tc.input).length===0)){
227	      d.appendChild(el('pre','tool-in', fmt(tc.input)));
228	    }
229	    var out=el('pre','tool-out'+(tc.isError?' err':''));
230	    if(tc.result!=null && tc.result!==''){ out.textContent=String(tc.result).slice(0,8000); } else { out.style.display='none'; }
231	    d.appendChild(out);
232	    byId('msgs').appendChild(d); scroll();
233	    return d;
234	  }
235	
236	  function select(s){
237	    cur=s; renderSidebar();
238	    // top shows BOTH: brief as the title (locate by task), first sentence as subtitle
239	    byId('h-title').textContent = s.brief ? s.brief.name : (s.title || s.project || 'session');
240	    byId('h-sub').textContent = s.title || (s.vendor+' · '+(s.cwd||''));
241	    // signals inline (no click needed): brief · pattern · priority · reason
242	    var sig=byId('h-sig'); sig.innerHTML='';
243	    sig.appendChild(el('span','briefref', s.brief ? ('task: '+s.brief.name) : 'no task brief'));
244	    if(s.pattern && s.pattern!=='unknown') sig.appendChild(el('span','pat '+s.pattern, s.pattern));
245	    if(s.score!=null) sig.appendChild(el('span','score','priority '+s.score.toFixed(1)));
246	    if(s.etaMin!=null) sig.appendChild(el('span','eta','~'+s.etaMin+'m to handle'));
247	    if(s.reason && s.reason!=='no signal') sig.appendChild(el('span','reason', s.reason));
248	    // in-browser fork only works for Claude sessions; Codex must use the terminal launcher
249	    var canFork = !!(s && s.sessionId && s.vendor==='claude' && !s.pendingFork);
250	    var ftitle = canFork ? 'branch this session into a fork'
251	      : (s && s.vendor!=='claude' ? 'in-browser split is Claude-only — use the terminal launcher for Codex' : 'select a Claude session to split');
252	    setForkEnabled(canFork, ftitle);
253	    byId('msgs').innerHTML=''; assistantEl=null;
254	    if(es){ es.close(); es=null; }
255	    if(s.pendingFork){
256	      addMsg('assistant','(forked — type your next message to continue this branch in a new direction)');
257	    } else if(s.file){
258	      fetch('/chat/messages?file='+encodeURIComponent(s.file)).then(function(r){return r.json();}).then(function(msgs){
259	        if(!msgs.length) addMsg('assistant','(no history yet)');
260	        msgs.forEach(function(m){ if(m.text) addMsg(m.role, m.text); (m.tools||[]).forEach(function(t){ addTool(t); }); });
261	      });
262	    }
263	  }
264	  function openStream(id){
265	    if(es) es.close();
266	    es=new EventSource('/chat/stream?session='+encodeURIComponent(id));
267	    es.onopen=function(){ setStatus(turnActive?'working':'live', turnActive?'working…':'live'); };
268	    es.onmessage=function(e){ onEvent(JSON.parse(e.data)); };
269	    es.onerror=function(){ setStatus('err', (es&&es.readyState===2)?'disconnected':'reconnecting…'); };
270	  }
271	  function onEvent(ev){
272	    if(ev.kind==='assistant_text'){ clearThinking(); if(!assistantEl) assistantEl=addMsg('assistant',''); var b=assistantEl.querySelector('.bubble'); b.textContent+=ev.text; scroll(); }
273	    else if(ev.kind==='tool_use'){ clearThinking(); assistantEl=null; var d=addTool({name:ev.name,input:ev.input}); if(ev.id) toolEls[ev.id]=d; }
274	    else if(ev.kind==='tool_result'){ assistantEl=null; var t=ev.id?toolEls[ev.id]:null;
275	      if(t){ var o=t.querySelector('.tool-out'); o.textContent=String(ev.text||'').slice(0,8000); o.style.display=''; if(ev.isError) o.className='tool-out err'; }
276	      else { addTool({name:'result',input:null,result:ev.text,isError:ev.isError}); }
277	      scroll(); }
278	    else if(ev.kind==='result'){ assistantEl=null; endTurn(); }
279	    else if(ev.kind==='error'){ clearThinking(); assistantEl=null; addMsg('error','⚠ '+ev.message); turnActive=false; var sb=byId('send'); if(sb) sb.disabled=false; setStatus('err','error'); }
280	  }
281	  function send(){
282	    var inp=byId('input'); var text=inp.value.trim(); if(!text||!cur) return;
283	    if(cur.pendingFork){ materializeFork(cur,text); return; }
284	    if(!cur.sessionId) return;
285	    addMsg('user',text); inp.value=''; assistantEl=null;
286	    turnActive=true; byId('send').disabled=true; startThinking();
287	    var id=cur.sessionId, cwd=cur.cwd||'';
288	    fetch('/chat/send?session='+encodeURIComponent(id)+'&cwd='+encodeURIComponent(cwd),{method:'POST',headers:{'content-type':'application/json'},body:JSON.stringify({text:text})})
289	      .then(function(r){return r.json();}).then(function(res){
290	        if(res.ok){ if(!es) openStream(id); }
291	        else { addMsg('error','⚠ '+(res.error||'send failed — this session may no longer be running')); endTurn(); }
292	      })
293	      .catch(function(e){ addMsg('error','⚠ network error: '+(e&&e.message?e.message:e)); endTurn(); });
294	  }
295	  function fork(){
296	    if(!cur||!cur.sessionId){ alert('Pick a session on the left before splitting.'); return; }
297	    if(cur.vendor!=='claude'){ alert('In-browser split is Claude-only. Use the terminal launcher to fork a Codex session.'); return; }
298	    if(cur.pendingFork){ byId('input').focus(); return; }
299	    // Open the branch immediately, empty and ready. The real fork is materialized
300	    // lazily on the first message: the Agent SDK only mints the new session id once
301	    // it has a turn to diverge on, so we defer the actual /chat/fork until send().
302	    // If you'd already typed something, that becomes the branch's opening turn —
303	    // "I typed this, but it's better off as a split" — and we materialize now.
304	    var inp=byId('input'); var text=inp.value.trim();
305	    var ns={vendor:'claude',sessionId:'pending-'+Date.now(),pendingFork:{parent:cur.sessionId,cwd:cur.cwd||''},title:'(fork) '+(cur.title||''),cwd:cur.cwd,project:cur.project,file:'',ageDays:0,prompts:0,actions:0,brief:cur.brief};
306	    SESS.unshift(ns); select(ns);
307	    if(text){ materializeFork(ns,text); } else { inp.focus(); }
308	  }
309	  // Turn a pending fork into a real session using its first message.
310	  function materializeFork(branch,text){
311	    var inp=byId('input'); byId('msgs').innerHTML=''; addMsg('user',text); inp.value=''; assistantEl=null;
312	    fetch('/chat/fork?session='+encodeURIComponent(branch.pendingFork.parent)+'&cwd='+encodeURIComponent(branch.pendingFork.cwd),
313	      {method:'POST',headers:{'content-type':'application/json'},body:JSON.stringify({text:text})})
314	      .then(function(r){return r.json();}).then(function(res){ if(!res.ok){ addMsg('error','⚠ '+(res.error||'fork failed')); return; }
315	        branch.sessionId=res.session; branch.pendingFork=null;
316	        if(cur===branch){ renderSidebar(); setForkEnabled(true,'branch this session into a fork'); }
317	        openStream(res.session); })
318	      .catch(function(e){ addMsg('error','⚠ fork failed: '+(e&&e.message?e.message:e)); });
319	  }
320	  function newSession(){
321	    var dir=byId('ndfree').value.trim()||byId('ndsel').value; var text=byId('np').value.trim();
322	    if(!dir){ byId('nmsg').textContent='pick or paste a directory'; return; }
323	    if(!text){ byId('nmsg').textContent='type a first message'; return; }
324	    fetch('/chat/new?cwd='+encodeURIComponent(dir),{method:'POST',headers:{'content-type':'application/json'},body:JSON.stringify({text:text})})
325	      .then(function(r){return r.json();}).then(function(res){ if(!res.ok){ byId('nmsg').textContent=res.error||'failed'; return; }
326	        var ns={vendor:'claude',sessionId:res.session,title:text,cwd:dir,project:(dir.split(/[\\\\/]/).pop()),file:'',ageDays:0,prompts:1,actions:0,brief:null};
327	        SESS.unshift(ns); select(ns); addMsg('user',text); openStream(res.session);
328	        byId('np').value=''; byId('nmsg').textContent=''; byId('newbox').classList.remove('open'); });
329	  }
330	
331	  sortSessions();
332	  renderSidebar();
333	  byId('send').onclick=send;
334	  byId('forkBtn').onclick=fork;
335	  byId('nbtn').onclick=newSession;
336	  byId('newToggle').onclick=function(){ byId('newbox').classList.toggle('open'); };
337	  byId('input').addEventListener('keydown',function(e){ if(e.key==='Enter'&&!e.shiftKey&&!e.isComposing){ e.preventDefault(); send(); } });
338	})();
339	</script>
340	</body>
341	</html>`;
342	}
```

```text
1	import { type AlignmentModel, scoreAlignment } from "./alignment.js";
2	import { classifyPattern } from "./pattern.js";
3	import type { Brief, Pattern, Telemetry } from "./types.js";
4	
5	export interface PriorityResult {
6	  score: number;
7	  reason: string;
8	  pattern: Pattern;
9	}
10	
11	/** Blocker present in `next`. Refines daemon.py's bare-"等" check so that a
12	 *  trailing "等" inside parentheticals (e.g. "(作品B 等)") no longer false-fires;
13	 *  "等" only counts when followed by concrete content. */
14	function hasBlocker(next: string): boolean {
15	  const lower = next.toLowerCase();
16	  if (lower.includes("block") || lower.includes("stuck") || next.includes("卡")) return true;
17	  return /等[^\s)）.。,，]/.test(next);
18	}
19	
20	function fmtDwell(minutes: number): string {
21	  return minutes >= 60 ? `${(minutes / 60).toFixed(1)}h` : `${Math.round(minutes)}m`;
22	}
23	
24	/** Cosine→score scale for memory alignment. Capped so a strong pattern signal
25	 *  (avoidance +4, stalled +3) still competes — alignment shouldn't dominate. */
26	const ALIGN_WEIGHT = 6;
27	const ALIGN_MIN_COSINE = 0.05;
28	
29	/**
30	 * Heuristic priority: memory alignment + pattern weight + blocker bonus −
31	 * defer/done penalty. Returns a composed, human-readable reason carrying the
32	 * evidence (DESIGN.md: no opaque scores — the user must be able to audit and
33	 * override the rank).
34	 */
35	export function evaluatePriority(
36	  brief: Brief,
37	  tel: Telemetry,
38	  model: AlignmentModel | null,
39	): PriorityResult {
40	  const blob = `${brief.name} ${brief.what} ${brief.next}`;
41	  let score = 0;
42	  const reasons: string[] = [];
43	
44	  if (model) {
45	    const align = scoreAlignment(model, blob);
46	    if (align.cosine >= ALIGN_MIN_COSINE) {
47	      score += align.cosine * ALIGN_WEIGHT;
48	      const terms = align.topTerms.length ? ` (${align.topTerms.join(", ")})` : "";
49	      reasons.push(`memory aligned${terms}`);
50	    }
51	  }
52	
53	  const pattern = classifyPattern(tel);
54	  if (pattern === "avoidance") {
55	    score += 4;
56	    reasons.push(
57	      `avoidance signal (${tel.prompts} prompts, 0 actions over ${fmtDwell(tel.totalMinutes)}) — needs decision, not work`,
58	    );
59	  } else if (pattern === "stalled") {
60	    score += 3;
61	    const touch = tel.lastTouchAgeDays !== null ? `, last touch ${tel.lastTouchAgeDays}d` : "";
62	    reasons.push(`stalled (${tel.prompts} prompts, 0 actions${touch}) — needs unblock or kill`);
63	  } else if (pattern === "fresh") {
64	    score += 1;
65	    reasons.push("fresh — no entries yet");
66	  } else if (pattern === "active") {
67	    score += 0.5;
68	  } else if (pattern === "healthy") {
69	    score -= 1;
70	    reasons.push("healthy — in flow, don't interrupt");
71	  }
72	
73	  if (brief.status === "deferred") {
74	    reasons.push(
75	      brief.deferUntil ? `deferred until ${brief.deferUntil}` : "deferred without condition",
76	    );
77	    score -= 2;
78	  } else if (brief.status === "done") {
79	    score = -100;
80	    reasons.push("done");
81	  }
82	
83	  if (hasBlocker(brief.next)) {
84	    score += 1;
85	    reasons.push("explicit blocker in next");
86	  }
87	
88	  return { score, reason: reasons.join("; ") || "no signal", pattern };
89	}
90
```

 `./\src\core\memory.ts`

```text
1	import fs from "node:fs";
2	import path from "node:path";
3	
4	const STOPWORDS = new Set([
5	  "the",
6	  "and",
7	  "with",
8	  "this",
9	  "that",
10	  "from",
11	  "have",
12	  "will",
13	  "name",
14	  "description",
15	  "metadata",
16	  "type",
17	  "memory",
18	  "file",
19	]);
20	
21	const CJK = /[一-鿿]{2,}/g;
22	const LATIN = /\b[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]{3,}\b/g;
23	
24	/**
25	 * Discover Claude Code per-project memory files. Memory model is unchanged from
26	 * Claude: each project keeps its own `memory/MEMORY.md` under
27	 * ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/. We union them as the user's memory corpus.
28	 */
29	export function discoverMemorySources(claudeProjects: string): string[] {
30	  const sources: string[] = [];
31	  let entries: fs.Dirent[];
32	  try {
33	    entries = fs.readdirSync(claudeProjects, { withFileTypes: true });
34	  } catch {
35	    return sources;
36	  }
37	  for (const entry of entries) {
38	    if (!entry.isDirectory()) continue;
39	    const mem = path.join(claudeProjects, entry.name, "memory", "MEMORY.md");
40	    if (fs.existsSync(mem)) sources.push(mem);
41	  }
42	  return sources;
43	}
44	
45	/** Read the raw text of each memory file (one document per file) for the alignment model. */
46	export function loadMemoryDocs(sources: string[]): string[] {
47	  const docs: string[] = [];
48	  for (const src of sources) {
49	    try {
50	      docs.push(fs.readFileSync(src, "utf-8"));
51	    } catch {
52	      // missing/unreadable memory file — skip
53	    }
54	  }
55	  return docs;
56	}
57	
58	/** Extract a deduped keyword set (CJK runs and latin words) from memory files. */
59	export function loadMemoryKeywords(sources: string[]): string[] {
60	  const keywords = new Set<string>();
61	  for (const src of sources) {
62	    let text: string;
63	    try {
64	      text = fs.readFileSync(src, "utf-8");
65	    } catch {
66	      continue;
67	    }
68	    for (const w of text.match(CJK) ?? []) keywords.add(w);
69	    for (const w of text.match(LATIN) ?? []) {
70	      if (!STOPWORDS.has(w.toLowerCase())) keywords.add(w);
71	    }
72	  }
73	  return [...keywords];
74	}
75
```

```text
1	import fs from "node:fs";
2	import path from "node:path";
3	import { serve } from "@hono/node-server";
4	import { Hono } from "hono";
5	import { streamSSE } from "hono/streaming";
6	import { ChatEngine } from "./chat/engine.js";
7	import { readClaudeTranscript } from "./chat/transcript.js";
8	import type { AttendConfig } from "./config.js";
9	import { type AlignmentModel, buildAlignmentModel } from "./core/alignment.js";
10	import { parseBrief, scanVault } from "./core/brief.js";
11	import { type LaunchAction, type LaunchVendor, launchSession } from "./core/launch.js";
12	import { discoverMemorySources, loadMemoryDocs } from "./core/memory.js";
13	import { evaluatePriority } from "./core/priority.js";
14	import { patternCounts, rankBriefs } from "./core/rank.js";
15	import { spawnCommand } from "./core/spawn.js";
16	import { matchSessions, telemetryForBrief } from "./core/telemetry.js";
17	import type { Brief, RankedBrief, RawSession, Telemetry } from "./core/types.js";
18	import { collectSessions } from "./core/vendor/index.js";
19	import { type ConsoleView, type SessionView, renderConsole } from "./ui/console.js";
20	import { renderDetail } from "./ui/detail.js";
21	import { renderFeed } from "./ui/feed.js";
22	
23	const DAY_MS = 86_400_000;
24	
25	/** Memoize an expensive scan so browser refreshes don't re-read every JSONL. */
26	function ttlCache<T>(ttlMs: number, fn: () => T): () => T {
27	  let stamp = 0;
28	  let value: T;
29	  let primed = false;
30	  return () => {
31	    const now = Date.now();
32	    if (primed && now - stamp < ttlMs) return value;
33	    value = fn();
34	    stamp = now;
35	    primed = true;
36	    return value;
37	  };
38	}
39	
40	/** Injectable so tests can assert wiring without spawning terminals or hitting the SDK. */
41	export interface AppDeps {
42	  launcher: (
43	    action: LaunchAction,
44	    vendor: LaunchVendor,
45	    cwd: string,
46	    opts: { sessionId?: string; prompt?: string },
47	  ) => string;
48	  engine: ChatEngine;
49	}
50	
51	/** Map each session to the highest-priority brief whose dir contains it (or vice versa). */
52	function briefBySession(ranked: RankedBrief[], sessions: RawSession[]): Map<string, RankedBrief> {
53	  const map = new Map<string, RankedBrief>();
54	  const sorted = [...ranked].sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
55	  for (const r of sorted) {
56	    for (const s of matchSessions(r.brief, sessions)) {
57	      if (!map.has(s.path)) map.set(s.path, r);
58	    }
59	  }
60	  return map;
61	}
62	
63	function knownDirs(ranked: RankedBrief[], sessions: RawSession[]): string[] {
64	  const set = new Set<string>();
65	  for (const r of ranked) set.add(path.resolve(r.brief.projectDir));
66	  for (const s of sessions) if (s.cwd) set.add(path.resolve(s.cwd));
67	  return [...set].filter((d) => fs.existsSync(d)).sort();
68	}
69	
70	/** Telemetry for a single session (so pattern/priority can be computed per-session). */
71	function sessionTelemetry(s: RawSession, now: number): Telemetry {
72	  const dwell = s.firstTs !== null && s.lastTs !== null ? (s.lastTs - s.firstTs) / 60_000 : null;
73	  const ageDays = s.lastTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - s.lastTs) / DAY_MS) : null;
74	  return {
75	    sessions: 1,
76	    prompts: s.prompts,
77	    actions: s.actions,
78	    totalMinutes: dwell ?? 0,
79	    avgSessionMin: dwell,
80	    lastActionAgeDays: s.actions > 0 ? ageDays : null,
81	    lastTouch: s.lastTs !== null ? new Date(s.lastTs).toISOString() : null,
82	    lastTouchAgeDays: ageDays,
83	  };
84	}
85	
86	function toSessionViews(
87	  sessions: RawSession[],
88	  ranked: RankedBrief[],
89	  model: AlignmentModel | null,
90	  now: number,
91	): SessionView[] {
92	  const byPath = briefBySession(ranked, sessions);
93	  return [...sessions]
94	    .sort((a, b) => (b.lastTs ?? 0) - (a.lastTs ?? 0))
95	    .map((s) => {
96	      const r = byPath.get(s.path);
97	      const tel = sessionTelemetry(s, now);
98	      // Per-session signals: treat the session's title as its "what" and score it
99	      // exactly like a brief, so pattern/priority/reason follow the session.
100	      const synthetic: Brief = {
101	        path: "",
102	        projectDir: s.cwd ?? "",
103	        name: s.title || (s.cwd ? path.basename(s.cwd) : "session"),
104	        frontMatter: {},
105	        what: s.title ?? "",
106	        accept: "",
107	        next: "",
108	        status: "active",
109	        deferUntil: null,
110	      };
111	      const { score, reason, pattern } = evaluatePriority(synthetic, tel, model);
112	      return {
113	        vendor: s.vendor,
114	        sessionId: s.sessionId,
115	        title: s.title ?? "",
116	        cwd: s.cwd,
117	        file: s.path,
118	        project: s.cwd ? path.basename(s.cwd) : "—",
119	        ageDays: s.lastTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - s.lastTs) / DAY_MS) : null,
120	        prompts: s.prompts,
121	        actions: s.actions,
122	        pattern,
123	        score,
124	        reason,
125	        etaMin: estimateEtaMin(
126	          s.lastTurnChars,
127	          s.lastTs !== null ? Math.floor((now - s.lastTs) / DAY_MS) : null,
128	        ),
129	        brief: r ? { name: r.brief.name, path: r.brief.path } : null,
130	      };
131	    });
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Estimate minutes to re-engage a session: re-read its last turn + re-orient
136	 * after being away. This is a property of the *session's transcript* (how much
137	 * to re-read + how stale), not the memory corpus — memory drives *priority*
138	 * (does this matter), the transcript drives *cost* (how long to handle it).
139	 */
140	function estimateEtaMin(lastTurnChars: number, ageDays: number | null): number {
141	  const readMin = lastTurnChars / 500; // ~500 chars/min to re-read dense text
142	  const reorientMin = Math.min(ageDays ?? 0, 14) * 0.7; // re-warming grows with staleness, capped
143	  return Math.max(1, Math.round(1 + readMin + reorientMin));
144	}
145	
146	export function createApp(
147	  config: AttendConfig,
148	  deps: AppDeps = { launcher: launchSession, engine: new ChatEngine() },
149	): Hono {
150	  const engine = deps.engine;
151	  const getSessions = ttlCache(30_000, () => collectSessions(config));
152	  const getModel = ttlCache(60_000, (): AlignmentModel => {
153	    const sources = config.memorySources.length
154	      ? config.memorySources
155	      : discoverMemorySources(config.claudeProjects);
156	    return buildAlignmentModel(loadMemoryDocs(sources));
157	  });
158	
159	  const app = new Hono();
160	
161	  // Main view: slock-style console — all sessions aggregated, chat in-browser.
162	  app.get("/", (c) => {
163	    const sessions = getSessions();
164	    const briefs = scanVault(config.vaultRoots, config.scanDepth);
165	    const ranked = rankBriefs(briefs, sessions, getModel());
166	    const view: ConsoleView = {
167	      sessions: toSessionViews(sessions, ranked, getModel(), Date.now()),
168	      knownDirs: knownDirs(ranked, sessions),

171	    };
172	    return c.html(renderConsole(view));
173	  });
174	
175	  // Static transcript of a session (history shown when you open it).
176	  app.get("/chat/messages", (c) => {

179	    return c.json(readClaudeTranscript(file));
180	  });
181	
182	  // Live event stream for a session (SSE). Replays buffered events, then streams.
183	  app.get("/chat/stream", (c) => {
184	    const id = c.req.query("session");
185	    if (!id) return c.text("missing session", 400);
186	    return streamSSE(c, async (stream) => {
187	      await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
188	        const unsub = engine.subscribe(id, (ev) => {
189	          stream.writeSSE({ data: JSON.stringify(ev) }).catch(() => {});
190	        });
191	        stream.onAbort(() => {
192	          unsub();
193	          resolve();
194	        });
195	      });
196	    });
197	  });
198	
199	  // Send a user turn; starts (resumes) a live run if one isn't already running.
200	  app.post("/chat/send", async (c) => {
201	    const id = c.req.query("session");
202	    const cwd = c.req.query("cwd");
203	    const body = (await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}))) as { text?: string };
204	    const text = body.text;
205	    if (!id) return c.json({ ok: false, error: "missing session" }, 400);
206	    if (!cwd || !fs.existsSync(cwd))
207	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "directory not found" }, 400);
208	    if (!text || !text.trim()) return c.json({ ok: false, error: "empty message" }, 400);
209	    if (!engine.get(id)) engine.start({ resume: id, cwd }).catch(() => {});
210	    const sent = engine.send(id, text);
211	    return c.json({ ok: sent, session: id });
212	  });
213	
214	  // Start a brand-new session in a directory.
215	  app.post("/chat/new", async (c) => {
216	    const cwd = c.req.query("cwd");
217	    const body = (await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}))) as { text?: string };
218	    const text = body.text;
219	    if (!cwd || !fs.existsSync(cwd))
220	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "directory not found" }, 400);
221	    if (!text || !text.trim()) return c.json({ ok: false, error: "empty message" }, 400);
222	    try {
223	      const session = await engine.start({ cwd, firstText: text });
224	      return c.json({ ok: true, session });
225	    } catch (err) {
226	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) }, 500);
227	    }
228	  });
229	
230	  // Fork (split) a session into a new branch. A fork needs a first turn to
231	  // diverge: the real Agent SDK only emits the new session id (in its init
232	  // message) once it receives input, so a fork with no first message would hang
233	  // forever waiting on an init that never comes.
234	  app.post("/chat/fork", async (c) => {
235	    const id = c.req.query("session");
236	    const cwd = c.req.query("cwd");
237	    const body = (await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}))) as { text?: string };
238	    const text = body.text;
239	    if (!id) return c.json({ ok: false, error: "missing session" }, 400);
240	    if (!cwd || !fs.existsSync(cwd))
241	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "directory not found" }, 400);
242	    if (!text || !text.trim())
243	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "type a message to branch with" }, 400);
244	    try {
245	      const session = await engine.start({ resume: id, forkSession: true, cwd, firstText: text });
246	      return c.json({ ok: true, session });
247	    } catch (err) {
248	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) }, 500);
249	    }
250	  });
251	
252	  // Brief-priority view (the original feed).
253	  app.get("/briefs", (c) => {

255	    const ranked = rankBriefs(briefs, getSessions(), getModel());
256	    return c.html(renderFeed(ranked, patternCounts(ranked), getModel().vocabSize));
257	  });
258	
259	  app.get("/brief", (c) => {
260	    const briefPath = c.req.query("path");
261	    if (!briefPath) return c.text("missing path", 400);
262	    if (!fs.existsSync(briefPath)) return c.text("not found", 404);
263	    const brief = parseBrief(briefPath);
264	    if (!brief) return c.text("parse failed", 500);
265	
266	    const sessions = getSessions();
267	    const telemetry = telemetryForBrief(brief, sessions);
268	    const { score, reason, pattern } = evaluatePriority(brief, telemetry, getModel());
269	    const matched = matchSessions(brief, sessions)
270	      .sort((a: RawSession, b: RawSession) => (b.lastTs ?? 0) - (a.lastTs ?? 0))
271	      .slice(0, 10);
272	
273	    return c.html(
274	      renderDetail({
275	        brief,
276	        telemetry,
277	        pattern,
278	        score,
279	        reason,
280	        sessions: matched,
281	        spawnClaude: spawnCommand(brief, "claude"),
282	        spawnCodex: spawnCommand(brief, "codex"),
283	      }),
284	    );
285	  });
286	
287	  // Launch a vendor action in a terminal: resume / fork an existing session, or start a new one.
288	  app.post("/launch", (c) => {
289	    const action = c.req.query("action");
290	    const vendor = c.req.query("vendor");
291	    const cwd = c.req.query("cwd");
292	    const id = c.req.query("id");
293	    const prompt = c.req.query("prompt");
294	
295	    if (action !== "resume" && action !== "fork" && action !== "new") {
296	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "unknown action" }, 400);
297	    }
298	    if (vendor !== "claude" && vendor !== "codex") {
299	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "unknown vendor" }, 400);
300	    }
301	    if (!cwd || !fs.existsSync(cwd)) {
302	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "directory not found" }, 400);
303	    }
304	    if ((action === "resume" || action === "fork") && (!id || !/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/.test(id))) {
305	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: "invalid session id" }, 400);
306	    }
307	    try {
308	      const command = deps.launcher(action, vendor, cwd, { sessionId: id, prompt });
309	      return c.json({ ok: true, command });
310	    } catch (err) {
311	      return c.json({ ok: false, error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) }, 500);
312	    }
313	  });
314	
315	  return app;
316	}
317	
318	export interface RunningServer {
319	  url: string;
320	  port: number;
321	  close: () => void;
322	}
323	
324	/**
325	 * Start the HTTP server; resolves once it is listening. If the port is already
326	 * in use, rolls forward to the next free port (up to `maxAttempts`) instead of
327	 * crashing — and logs the bump so the printed URL is always the real one.
328	 */
329	export function startServer(config: AttendConfig, maxAttempts = 10): Promise<RunningServer> {
330	  const app = createApp(config);
331	  const listen = (port: number, attemptsLeft: number): Promise<RunningServer> =>
332	    new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
333	      const server = serve({ fetch: app.fetch, hostname: config.host, port }, () => {
334	        resolve({ url: `http://${config.host}:${port}`, port, close: () => server.close() });
335	      });
336	      server.on("error", (err: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
337	        if (err.code === "EADDRINUSE" && attemptsLeft > 0) {
338	          process.stderr.write(`port ${port} in use, trying ${port + 1}…\n`);
339	          resolve(listen(port + 1, attemptsLeft - 1));
340	        } else {
341	          reject(err);
342	        }
343	      });
344	    });
345	  return listen(config.port, maxAttempts - 1);
346	}
347
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	import type { Pattern, Telemetry } from "./types.js";
2	
3	/**
4	 * `avoidance` requires not just many prompts with no actions, but a *sustained*
5	 * one. This operationalizes Rosenbaum's pre-crastination caveat (a short
6	 * easy-task detour to free working memory is healthy; only a long, output-less
7	 * stretch looks like decision-avoidance) and keeps a brief, output-less planning
8	 * session from being mislabeled (PM-A: a single false "avoidance" call destroys
9	 * trust in every badge). Uses task-internal dwell — not recency — so it does not
10	 * reintroduce the rejected hot/warm/cold-by-time model.
11	 */
12	export const AVOIDANCE_MIN_MINUTES = 60;
13	
14	/**
15	 * Map telemetry to a behavioral pattern. Labels are descriptive observations,
16	 * never verdicts (DESIGN.md invariant 3, Steel 2007).
17	 */
18	export function classifyPattern(tel: Telemetry): Pattern {
19	  const { sessions, actions, prompts, avgSessionMin, lastTouchAgeDays, totalMinutes } = tel;
20	  if (sessions === 0) return "fresh";
21	  if (prompts >= 5 && actions === 0 && totalMinutes >= AVOIDANCE_MIN_MINUTES) return "avoidance";
22	  if (actions === 0 && lastTouchAgeDays !== null && lastTouchAgeDays >= 7) return "stalled";
23	  if (
24	    actions > 0 &&
25	    avgSessionMin !== null &&
26	    avgSessionMin >= 10 &&
27	    lastTouchAgeDays !== null &&
28	    lastTouchAgeDays <= 3
29	  ) {
30	    return "healthy";
31	  }
32	  if (actions > 0) return "active";
33	  return "unknown";
34	}
35
```

```text
7:A single person collaborating with AI faces four compounding costs:
12:4. **Polling overhead** — N IDE tabs × re-orientation cost on each switch (UC Irvine: 23 min 15 s to refocus; APA: 40% productivity loss from switching). Adding more organization tools makes this worse, not better.
36:  - `next` — **where you are stuck / what to try next**. Update this line before every session-leave. Gollwitzer's implementation-intention research: the meta-decision ("what should I do?") is where cost concentrates; pre-committing it at cheap time pays off massively.
59:### 3. Priority evaluator
111:- **Hot/warm/cold by time**. Session state changes fast but total count stays high; recency doesn't track priority. Replaced by memory-aligned semantic priority + pattern-based action implication.
112:- **LangChain Agent Inbox / push notifications**. User's diagnostic cost when polling is already near-zero; push adds noise without enabling action.
113:- **Cap to 2-5 concurrent sessions**. Open ≠ active. Goal isn't reducing tab count, it's reducing re-warming cost via durable briefs.
115:- **Auto-spawn from dashboard**. Auto-spawning new sessions from priority list would recreate the cascade we just dismantled. Spawn buttons copy commands only. *(Partially overridden in v1.2 — see below: a manual per-session "split ⑂" button now actively launches a vendor `fork` in a terminal. The override is narrow — one explicit click forks one existing session — and does not auto-spawn from the ranked list, so the cascade concern stays addressed.)*
124:Everything in between (briefs, dashboard, priority, telemetry) is vendor-agnostic markdown/HTML.
146:- [ ] LLM-based priority option (env-flag) — **deferred** until the legible heuristic is demonstrably insufficient on real briefs; do not trade the auditable one-line reason for an opaque score.
181:**What did NOT change.** All five design invariants hold. The memory model is unchanged — still Claude Code's per-project memory (`~/.claude/projects/*/memory/MEMORY.md`), just generalized from one hardcoded file to auto-discovering and unioning all per-project memories. The pattern definitions, priority heuristic, descriptive-telemetry constraint, and spawn-by-copy behavior are ported 1:1 (one refinement: the bare-"等" blocker false-positive from the roadmap is fixed in `priority.ts`).
202:`split` was originally specced as an LLM step (transcript → N candidate briefs). The user redefined it: **no LLM** — a manual per-session **"split ⑂" button** on the brief detail page that branches that session via the vendor's own fork command, run in a new terminal:
208:[Omitted long matching line]
212:The user clarified the product they actually want, and it differs from the original brief-centric pull dashboard: **a session command center** — open it and see *all my chats aggregated across vendors*, with brief + priority attached, and act on them directly (continue / split / new). This is a real reversal of two original stances ("brief is the primary object, session is the derived cache"; "not a chat UI / not a launcher"). The user owns the design; we followed the redirect.
215:- **Main view (`/`) is now sessions-primary.** It lists every session from `~/.claude/projects` and `~/.codex/sessions` (not just brief-matched ones), newest first, each showing a title (first user prompt), vendor, project dir, prompts/actions, age, and — when its cwd matches a brief — that brief's name + priority score + pattern (linking to the brief detail). The brief-priority feed moved to `/briefs`.
218:What is retained: briefs, priority, telemetry, the descriptive-not-judgmental constraint, cross-vendor neutrality, and the single localhost surface all still hold — sessions are now the lens, briefs the attached signal. The launcher is the v1.2 invariant override, generalized: attend now actively starts/continues/forks sessions (still one explicit click each; no auto-spawn of a queue).
220:**Tension to keep in mind (PM-B):** a sessions-list + launcher overlaps Claude Code's Agent View / Conductor. attend's remaining differentiation is the *union* — cross-vendor + behavioral brief/priority attached to each session — not the session list itself.
```
